name: Nan city center
notes: Provincial capital and historic city with Nan River, museums, and temples.

name: Nan Bus Terminal
notes: Main bus terminal in Nan city.

name: Nan Airport (Nan Nakhon Airport)
notes: Airport east of Nan city; domestic flights from Bangkok.

name: Wat Phra That Chae Haeng
notes: Major Nan temple and tourism anchor.

name: Wat Phra That Khao Noi
notes: Hilltop temple with city view; tourism anchor.

name: Nan National Museum
notes: Important historical museum in former Nan city palace.

name: Big C Nan
notes: Major retail anchor west of Nan city center.

name: Lotus's Nan
notes: Supermarket anchor on west side of Nan.

name: Wiang Sa district center / Route 101
notes: Major south district and Route 101 corridor hub with 57,734 population.

name: Pua district center / tourism gateway
notes: North Nan district and tourism gateway to Doi Phu Kha National Park; 44,326 population.

name: Tha Wang Pha district center
notes: District center north of Nan city on Route 1081 corridor; 35,414 population.

name: Na Noi district center / Route 101
notes: South Nan district on Route 101; 23,668 population; important corridor stop.

name: Doi Phu Kha National Park
notes: Major mountain tourism destination in far north Nan.

name: Chiang Klang district center
notes: North Nan district; 19,614 population; mountain coverage.

name: Song Khwae district center
notes: East Nan district; 8,759 population; remote coverage.
